summary: Excellent matrix mix captures an extraordinary show highlighted by a soaring Eyes opener, epic Dark Star and standout Bruce Hornsby contributions.
review: The Dusborne matrix delivers crystal‑clear balance of soundboard and audience sources, earning praise as “perfect” and “right on the money.”  The performance itself is celebrated for its electrifying Eyes opener, a monster Dark Star, tight Truckin' fills from Bruce Hornsby, and memorable moments like Loose Lucy and New Speedway, making this one of the best shows of the early‑’90 tour.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviewers give 4–5 stars and highlight both an outstanding musical performance and a flawless matrix recording, indicating an exceptional show with excellent technical quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix of soundboard (SBD) and audience (AUD) described as "right on the money," "perfect," and "sounds wonderful" with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a cohesive flow, with the powerful Eyes opener setting the tone and seamless transitions into extended jams; Bruce Hornsby’s piano adds fresh energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry was especially energized playing with Bruce Hornsby, enjoying the interplay and song choices.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby is praised for tasteful fills on Truckin' and making the band feel fresh; Vince’s keyboard work is called underrated but growing, adding high harmonies alongside Bruce.
